data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify employs a microservices architecture to enable independent deployment and scaling of individual services.
data.technical_spec: Each service is designed to perform a specific task, communicated via RESTful APIs.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: The system leverages event-driven components for asynchronous communication between services.
data.technical_spec: Uses message brokers like Kafka to handle streaming data and events.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A CDN is used to efficiently serve media content to users worldwide, reducing latency and bandwidth usage.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates globally distributed servers to deliver audio streams and assets.

data.title: Hybrid Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Spotify operates a hybrid cloud model to balance costs, performance, and geographical reach.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes both on-premises servers and cloud services from providers like Google Cloud.

data.title: Database Sharding
data.description: To manage scale and performance, Spotify implements database sharding across its data storage systems.
data.technical_spec: Divide databases into smaller, manageable pieces called shards, tailored for horizontal scaling.
